#Summary1=(human1) In letters from 1962-1963, James P. Cannon denies claims by Francis Russel in a book and in the New Republic that he believed and privately stated that Sacco and Vanzetti, who had been executed decades earlier, were guilty, and corresponds with Pennsylvania Supreme Court Justic Michael A. Musmanno, who applauds Cannon and concurs the two were innocent and Russel is unreliable.

#Summary2=(claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929; postedited) In a  1962/1963 letter exchange with Pennsylvania Supreme Court Justice Michael A. Musmanno, James P. Cannon refutes Francis Russell's published claim that he privately admitted thinking Sacco was guilty, asserting he always believed in Sacco and Vanzetti's innocence and criticizing Russell's careless historical methods.

#Summary3=(gpt4o) In a letter to the New Republic, James P. Cannon refutes Francis Russell's claim that he believed Sacco was guilty, affirming his long-held stance on Sacco and Vanzetti's innocence, while Justice Michael A. Musmanno commends Cannon for his clarity and provides further criticism of Russell's historical inaccuracies.
#Summary4=(Meta-Llama-3-8B-Instruct) James P. Cannon disputes Francis Russell's claim that he privately admitted Sacco's guilt, stating he has always believed the pair were innocent.

#Summary5=(Qwen2.5-7B-Instruct) James P. Cannon writes to the New Republic and others to refute Francis Russell's claims about his thoughts on the Sacco and Vanzetti case, providing evidence of his belief in their innocence and disputing Russell's historical inaccuracies.
#Text=New Republic , Aug 27 , 1962
#Text=What Cannon Did n’t Think
#Text=Sirs :

#Text=I was astounded to read the statement by Francis Russell in your correspondence column of March 23 that “ James Cannon who organized the International Labor Defense to aid Sacco and Vanzetti admitted privately that he thought Sacco was guilty . ” ( Emphasis added . )
#Text=After reading this revelation of my private thoughts about a case that began 43 years ago , I managed to get a copy of Mr. Russell ’s book Tragedy in Dedham which I had previously missed .
#Text=There I found on page 332 that Mr. Russell had explained my attitude somewhat differently , as follows :

#Text=“ After his expulsion from the party , James Cannon , the International Labor Defense ’s executive secretary , was to admit privately — much as Moore did — that he felt Sacco was guilty . ” ( Emphasis added . )
#Text=The truth is that I have never felt or thought that Sacco was guilty .
#Text=I have always thought they were innocent , and have never expressed a different thought or feeling , privately or publicly , anywhere at any time .
#Text=I would like to add a word in defense of the memory of Carlo Tresca which has been besmirched by Mr. Russell ’s statement that Tresca privately told Max Eastman “ that Sacco was guilty . ”
#Text=I knew Carlo Tresca very well and was closely associated with him in the campaign for Sacco and Vanzetti , and for 25 years after that .

#Text=We collaborated over the years in many activities of common interest during which we had occasion to visit each other ’s offices , to drink wine together at Italian restaurants , and to talk of many things in friendly , casual conversation .
#Text=It would not be too much to say that we were friends .
#Text=Never , at any time , did I ever hear him express or even intimate any doubt about the innocence of Sacco and Vanzetti .
#Text=And I never heard any report , or rumor , or gossip , from anyone else who ever heard such a thing about Tresca until Mr. Russell ’s statement hit me in the eye .
#Text=James P. Cannon
#Text=Los Angeles , Calif.
#Text=SUPREME COURT OF PENNSYLVANIA
#Text=Justice Michael A. Musmanno 811 City - County Building Pittsburgh 19 , Pennsylvania
#Text=May 13 , 1963
#Text=Mr. James P. Cannon 1902 Hyperion Ave. Los Angeles 27 , California
#Text=Dear Mr. Cannon :
#Text=I congratulate you on that wonderful letter you wrote to the New Republic , repudiating Francis Russell ’s statement about you in connection with the Sacco - Vanzetti case .
#Text=You undoubtedly know I wrote a review of Russell ’s book , in which I uncovered other misstatements of his .
#Text=I enclose a copy of it herewith just in case you did not see it .
#Text=I would be grateful if you would write me telling just how Russell got around to misquoting and misrepresenting you .
#Text=In appreciation of your reply , I am ,
#Text=Sincerely yours ,
#Text=( signed M. A. Musmanno )
#Text=P.S. I enclose herewith also copy of the speech I made in Massachusetts when I was supporting the bill for a posthumous pardon for the two cruelly executed innocent men .
#Text=James P. Cannon 1902 Hyperion Ave. Los Angeles 27 , Calif.
#Text=May 23 , 1963
#Text=Justice Michael A. Musmanno 811 City - County Building Pittsburgh 19 , Pennsylvania
#Text=Dear Justice Musmanno :
#Text=I received your letter of May 13th with the two enclosures .
#Text=I especially appreciated receiving a copy of your speech before the Massachusetts Legislature , as reprinted in the Congressional Record .

#Text=I had read your review of Francis Russell ’s book at the time it appeared in the New Republic and referred to it in a paragraph of my letter to the editor , but this paragraph was eliminated in the editing .
#Text=( A carbon copy of my original letter is enclosed . )
#Text=I salute your speech and review as mighty contributions to the counter-offensive against the defamers of Sacco ’s memory .

#Text=Mr. Russell is obviously a very careless historian : he did not bother to check with me before rushing into print with a completely false statement , attributing to me an opinion about Sacco ’s guilt , which I never held and never expressed anywhere .
#Text=The case is made all the worse for Mr. Russell by the fact that he had written me under date of Oct. 7 , 1960 .
#Text=This letter ( reproduced from New York ) dealt with a wild rumor he had heard somewhere or other about a matter of which I had no knowledge and did not bother to deny .
#Text=There was no inquiry as to my opinion about Sacco ’s guilt or innocence .
#Text=( For your information I enclose the original of Mr. Russell ’s Oct. 7 , 1960 letter .
#Text=This is the only communication I received from him . )
#Text=Sincerely yours ,
#Text=James P. Cannon
#Text=JPC : dn encl. ( 2 )
#Text=Supreme Court of Pennsylvania
#Text=Justice Michael A. Musmanno 811 City - County Building Pittsburgh 19 , Pennsylvania
#Text=May 27 , 1963
#Text=Mr. James P. Cannon 1902 Hyperion Avenue Los Angeles 27 , California
#Text=Dear Mr. Cannon :
#Text=I was delighted to receive your letter and appreciate your submitting further proof of Francis Russell ’s deceptions , trickery and general unreliability .
#Text=Please let me know if I may quote you in connection with your repudiation of Russell ’s statements concerning you .
#Text=I am sending you with this letter my full - length review of Russell ’s book , printed in the current issue of the Kansas Law Review .
#Text=I am sure you will find it interesting .
#Text=I am sorry I did not know , as I was writing the review , of the manner in which Russell treated you .
#Text=I certainly would have given him a blast on it .
#Text=With renewed expression of gratitude for your letter and cooperation in the cause of truth , I am , with best wishes ,
#Text=Sincerely yours ,
#Text=Signed —
#Text=Michael Musmanno
